back_translation: There shall not be to the Levitical priests, the whole tribe of Levi, a portion and inheritance with Israel; the fire offerings of the Lord and His inheritance they shall eat.
And inheritance shall not be to him in the midst of his brothers; the Lord, He is his inheritance, as He spoke to him.
And this shall be the right of the priests from the people, from those who sacrifice the sacrifice, whether ox or sheep: he shall give to the priest the shoulder and the cheeks and the stomach.
The firstfruits of your grain, your new wine, and your oil, and the first of the fleece of your flock you shall give to him.
For in him the Lord your God has chosen out of all your tribes, to stand to minister in the name of the Lord, he and his sons all the days.
And when the Levite comes from one of your gates from all Israel, where he sojourns there, and he comes in all the desire of his soul to the place that the Lord will choose,
then he shall minister in the name of the Lord his God like all his brothers the Levites who stand there before the Lord.
Portion like portion they shall eat, apart from his sales upon the fathers.
When you come to the land that the Lord your God is giving to you, you shall not learn to do according to the abominations of those nations.
There shall not be found among you one who makes his son or his daughter pass through the fire, a diviner of divinations, one who practices omens, or a sorcerer, or a caster of spells,
or one who binds a binding, or one who consults a medium and spiritist, or one who inquires of the dead.
For an abomination of the Lord is everyone who does these things, and because of these abominations the Lord your God is driving them out from before you.
Blameless you shall be with the Lord your God.
For these nations whom you will dispossess listen to soothsayers and to diviners, and as for you, not so has the Lord your God given to you.
A prophet from your midst, from your brothers, like me, the Lord your God will raise up for you; to him you shall listen.

According to all that you asked from the Lord your God at Horeb, on the day of the assembly, saying: Let me not continue to hear the voice of the Lord my God, and this great fire let me not see again, and let me not die.
And the Lord said to me: They have done well what they have spoken.
A prophet I will raise up for them from the midst of their brothers, like you, and I will put My words in his mouth, and he shall speak to them all that I command him.
And it shall be, the man who does not listen to My words that he shall speak in My name, I will require from him.
But the prophet who acts presumptuously to speak a word in My name that I have not commanded him to speak, or that he speaks in the name of other gods, that prophet shall die.
And when you say in your heart: How shall we know the word that the Lord has not spoken?

What the prophet speaks in the name of the Lord, and the word does not happen and does not come, that is the word the Lord has not spoken; with presumption the prophet has spoken it; you shall not fear from him.
